title: 融合 harness-engineering 资料丰富指南
stageGoal: 将 harness-engineering 目录中的关键定义与实践整合进 Harness-runner 指南，使指南同时说明插件定位、使用流程和 Harness 的工程化含义。
summary: Completed US-011 by enriching the existing guide chapter model with harness-engineering definitions, failure modes, and guardrail concepts, then mapping those ideas onto current Harness Runner artifacts and workflows without changing the command surface.
architectureNotes: The guide remains a content concern inside src/helpManual.ts. No new command registration, menu routing, or localization structure was introduced for this story.
The new harness-engineering chapter is a semantic expansion of the current chapter model, so future handoffs can keep extending content without coupling guide evolution to extension.ts changes.

Reviewer focus should stay on concept-to-capability mapping quality: the story is successful only if the added harness terminology matches existing artifacts, gates, and reviewer flows instead of inventing parallel product behavior.
If future scope requires source metadata, add it to the chapter model as a narrow extension point rather than splitting guide rendering into a second service.
keyDecisions: Keep the single Harness-runner guide entry and absorb new material into chapters instead of creating a separate harness-engineering help command.
Add one dedicated chapter for harness engineering meaning, drift risks, and guardrail mapping.
Update the guide regression test to assert the newly integrated narrative rather than the old future-ingestion placeholder copy.
confirmedConstraints: Changes stayed inside guide/test files and story-scoped .harness-runner artifacts.
At least one relevant validation command was executed before completion.
Commit should use Chinese conventional commit wording.
unresolvedRisks: No live VS Code webview end-to-end test was added for the enriched guide content.
Future reviewer may still ask for source attribution metadata if they want stronger traceability from guide text back to harness-engineering references.
nextStoryPrerequisites: If the next story extends guide traceability, decide whether source metadata belongs on chapters, sections, or both before editing the renderer.
If a future story changes guide navigation, preserve the current single-entry model unless the chapter structure proves insufficient.
resumeRecommendation: US-011 is complete. Resume from src/helpManual.ts and the guide regression test if a reviewer requests stronger source attribution, alternate phrasing for guardrails, or additional webview-level validation.
